architecture_fit.note: Minimal 2-layer single-head attention (prev-token + content-match), no MLP — a textbook induction circuit that directly solves substring matching at 0.978 detection.

baseline_comparison.note: Explicit strawman: zeroing the Layer-0 prev-token write collapses detection to 0.000, plotted against the random baseline (0.016).

faithfulness.note: Causal ablation of the prev-token head drops detection to chance, showing the match depends on the circuit and not on reading source/target positions.

operating_range.note: Holds in [0.90,1.0] across all plen×dist cells, but distance only spans 8→32 (~4x); README's '2 orders of magnitude' claim overstates the fixed sweep.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set torch weights on CUDA with no training — exactly the hand-built circuit this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab pairs a circuit/ablation/baseline bar chart, a plen×dist table, and the raw Layer-1 attention bar with source_pos highlighted — claim is legible in one frame.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies each panel as the smallest falsifiable artefact and ties axes/baseline directly to the goal's question.

notes: A clean, faithful hand-built two-layer induction circuit that is a minimal delta from base_model.py and scores 0.978 detection with a clear prev-head ablation collapsing to chance. Baseline contrast, hand-set weights, and visualisation are all strong; only the operating-range claim is slightly oversold given the fixed ~4x distance sweep. Mean of scored items (4.86) places it in the 'good' band.
